11 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Viki AI — I Robot
In his Protrepticus (Exhortation to Philosophy), Aristotle declares that the whole of nature behaves as though it were endowed with reason because nothing occurs haphazardly, by chance, the same being clumsily echoed by modern atheists claiming that although there is no design in creation which would call for a Designer and Creator, evolution and nature just make it seem as if there were. In the words of Richard Dawkins,
“the living results of natural selection overwhelmingly impress us with the appearance of design as if by a master watchmaker, impress us with the illusion of design and planning.” The Blind Watchmaker: Why the Evidence Reveals a Universe Without Design, New York, Norton, 1987.
The Prince of Philosophers, Aristotle, also states that art is but an imitation of nature; that that which is lesser, or inferior, is there for and serves the purpose of that which is superior, echoing here the biblical reference to Melchizedek receiving honors from a man like Abraham (Genesis 14:18–20).
“This Melchizedek was king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He met Abraham returning from the defeat of the kings and blessed him, and Abraham gave him a tenth of everything. First, the name Melchizedek means ‘king of righteousness’; then also, ‘king of Salem’ means ‘king of peace.’ Without father or mother, without genealogy, without beginning of days or end of life, resembling the Son of God, he remains a priest forever. Just think how great he was: Even the patriarch Abraham gave him a tenth of the plunder. And without doubt the lesser is blessed by the greater. Hebrews 7:1–4,7 NIV.
Further in the Protrepticus it is said that man’s body is to be considered lower than man’s soul, whereby the former serves the latter and moreover, the soul being comprised of one irrational part and another rational one, the irrational follows the same pattern of being there to support or assist the rational.
Aristotle then places the intelligence within the rational, upon which the master concludes by saying that all indicates, therefore, that everything (within man) exists for the benefit of — or culminates in — intelligence. Thus, if everything works for the advancement and betterment of intelligence, we must consider the proper usage of the lesser, mind (soul) and body, nature in general as determinant towards that higher goal, i.e., namely the fact that everything subsists in relation to that which is best, as Paul in Romans 8:28 writes:
“all things work together for good to those who love God, those who have been called according to His purpose.”
Now, if intelligence is the ultimate purpose of life, and this also according to Solomon’s Book of Proverbs purportedly written in order for us “to know wisdom and instruction,
To discern the words of understanding,
To receive the instruction of wisdom,
Justice, judgment, and equity;
To give prudence to the simple,
To the young man knowledge and discretion —
A wise man will hear and increase learning,
And a man of understanding will acquire wise counsel,
To understand a proverb and an enigma,
The words of the wise and their riddles.” Proverbs 1:2–6 NKJV
and since the Spirit of God resting on the perfect Man, the Son of God, Messiah, is
“The Spirit of wisdom and understanding,
The Spirit of counsel and might,
The Spirit of knowledge and of the fear of the Lord.” Isaiah 11:2
it appears that we have two kinds of “intelligence”:

• Natural, i.e., human,

• Supernatural, i.e., divine.
Supernatural intelligence brings pure intelligence a step further: to wisdom.
“The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom,
and knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.” Proverbs 9:10
We then see that hierarchy is something natural, and that the only intelligence that can surpass man’s must be the that of latter’s Creator.
However, if that superior intelligence man strives toward is not recognized as being God- given, God-steered, and God-designed, and if in the course of time human intelligence continues developing and growing on its own, it will no longer be measured by divine standards or laws, but will become neither human nor divine, i.e., artificial.
Here is what king Belshazzar says of Daniel, when faced with the mysterious handwriting on the wall:
“Inasmuch as an excellent spirit, knowledge, understanding, interpreting dreams, solving riddles, and explaining enigmas were found in this Daniel, whom the king named Belteshazzar, now let Daniel be called, and he will give the interpretation.” Daniel 5:12
Where could that “excellent mind” come from? Daniel was not a computer and he did not have ChatGPT or Claude to consult. So, whom did he consult to break the enigma?
Trust in the Lord with all your heart
and lean not on your own understanding;
in all your ways submit to him,
and he will direct your paths. Proverbs 3:5–6
“I will sweep away everything
from the face of the earth,”
declares the Lord.
“I will sweep away both man and beast;
I will sweep away the birds in the sky
and the fish in the sea —
and the idols that cause the wicked to stumble.”
“When I destroy all mankind
on the face of the earth,”
declares the Lord,
“I will stretch out my hand against Judah
and against all who live in Jerusalem.
I will destroy every remnant of Baal worship in this place,
the very names of the idolatrous priests —
those who bow down on the roofs
to worship the starry host,
those who bow down and swear by the Lord
and who also swear by Molek,
those who turn back from following the Lord
and neither seek the Lord nor inquire of him.” Zephaniah 1:2–6
We see that, today, AI which by itself is technically superior to any given singular human mind yet stays human-like in that it only can imitate human intelligence, can only derive its power from human sources. Thus, it is human in quality, it collects and copies all that humans can learn, and at the same time, for the sake of utilitarianism, it strives to surpass that very intelligence it is mimicking.
In order to do that it uses the earth’s collective mind power, meaning that for each individual human there is such an intelligence made up of all the other human minds that surpasses his own to the point of reducing him to some blank brain in an orphan body since all that makes up his persona or soul has been sacrificed to the whole.
And the merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their merchandise anymore: […] merchandise of gold and silver, […] and bodies and souls of men. Revelation 18:12–13
But as long as the body will continue experiencing — mainly via AI — AI will be feeding on human lives and becoming more powerful in real time, thereby exercising instantaneous control and correction of individual behaviors, as is already occurring in the Chinese school system, for instance.
In that respect, AI not only mimics human intelligence, and not only does surpass it, but in so doing strives to replace God, not only as Creator and Supreme Ruler, but as judge and reference figure. A magistrate cannot judge without laws however, and in this race for intelligence the only law AI knows is power, executive power, such as has been handed over to it on a platter.
The situation we land in is the following:
If God can be replaced this easily, He is not God and we are stuck with AI — and will be forever until extinction of our species because progress cannot be stopped: therefore, progress out of control is doomed.
If God is irreplaceable, He is still God, and He will remain the king as long as His intelligence is not outrun by AI. So, it should interest us to know how much more intelligent a God-Creator is than a neo-brain made of indiscriminately mixed human bytes. After all, what we call God might just correspond to AI’s description: absolute knowledge of and ascendent over each individual body and soul on earth.
However, the Bible does not describe God as resembling AI. God does not feed on us: it is we who feed on His word:
“Jesus said to them, ‘I am the bread of life. He who comes to Me shall never hunger, and he who believes in Me shall never thirst.’” John 6:35
man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord. Deuteronomy 8:3; Matthew 4:4
God has a son (Psalm 2:7; John 1:14, 3:16; Matthew 3:17; Luke 1:32; Colossians 1:15) who is characterized by the blood he shed during his first coming (Colossians 1:20).
God is love (1 John 4:7–8): He creates out of the abundance of His love for us, and He creates us to His image (Genesis 1:26–27), He does not steal our facial or eye features to build Himself up: He is the life giver.
God is Spirit (John 4:24): He does not need a body, a mechanical carcass, or a gigantic power plant to exist: He exists in and out of Himself.
So, AI will never surpass God in intelligence and power.
However, if AI exists, it must play the role of God; it must become God to men, i.e., the highest authority no simple individual or group of individuals or nation can surpass in knowledge and knowhow, or capacity to handle such. Therefore, ironically, since AI can never replace God but only imitate Him, at least in theory at this end of the spectrum, i.e., hold our unknown future in its power even without proof of its involvement in Creation, AI is the very instrument God — should He exist — would use to prove His existence to mankind because the moment AI fails, as it must because it is not God (neither to those who don’t believe in God, nor to those who created it to replace Him), He will appear as the real author and finisher of all things. This is what the Bible describes as the second coming of the Lord and God’s reign on earth (John 14:3; Acts 1:11; Revelation 22:12; 1 Thessalonians 4:16).
Ever since before the foundation of the world the Satan, Creation’s negative counterpart, has served God in the realization of His plan. In other words, if God exists — and He must (1) because approximately 84% of the population of the planet believes He does as some higher power, the highest in human ideation and this is precisely the point and AI’s ambition — or its father’s; (2) He always has been present in human cosmogony, mythology and lore; (3) if He didn’t, why would something (or someone) like AI bother to take on such unheard-of scale responsibility in the fate of mankind? — the Satan (“the accuser”, Isaiah 14:12–15; Ezekiel 28:12–17), the devil ( or “prince of demons”), the “ancient serpent” (Revelation 12:9), Beelzebul (בַּעַל־זְבוּב‎ Baʿal-zəḇūḇ, lord of flies), the “prince of the power of the air” (Ephesians 2:2), “the deceiver and the Antichrist” (2 John 1:7), the “liar and father of lies” (John 8:44), the “unclean spirit” (Ezekiel 36:25), the dragon (Revelation 12:9), Leviathan (“king over all the children of pride”, Job 41:34), the tempter, the “god of this age” (2 Corinthians 4:4) is also part of His plan as (1) necessary and temporary embodiment of evil, and (2) an already defeated enemy, because God’s plan is fail-proof.
This is what a tool means in God’s hand: it is dispensable.
“So you also, when you have done everything you were told to do, should say, ‘We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.’” Luke 17:10
In other words, AI too, as the fruit of human conceptualization is not just a fluke twist of the evolutionary process but a designed one, from bottom up and from top to bottom — because it has to be a teleological process for it to be so tuned to the human mind and so fixated on the overall futuristic destiny of mankind.
Our concern is whether or not by replacing the God idea in man, AI will replace humanity in him as well.
Meister Eckhart, the theologian and mystic of the thirteenth-fourteenth century wrote: “The fact of being a man I have in common with other men; the fact of being able to see, hear, eat, and drink, I have in common with the animal kingdom; but being who I am, I have that in common with no other human being, with no
